message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "CLI writes payout and remainingCap integers for a claim step result, in step order"
Expected behavior: src/cli.ts must handle claim steps. A claim step has {op:"claim", policy:<index>, incident}. It must call claim(policyObject, incident) where policyObject is the QuoteResult returned by the quote step at that zero-based step index. The same QuoteResult object must be reused (it carries items and the mutable remainingCap). Push {payout, remainingCap} to results for claim steps, {premium} for quote steps. Results must be in step order.

For the active test: quote amulet (step 0), then claim against policy 0 with amulet damage 200 → payout 100, remainingCap 1100.

Implementation: keep an array of the QuoteResult objects indexed by step index (or store each step's policy). For each step: if op==="quote", call quote() and store the QuoteResult, push {premium}; if op==="claim", look up the stored QuoteResult at step.policy, call claim(policy, step.incident), push {payout, remainingCap}.

Keep all 35 prior tests green. Do NOT implement error/validation cases yet (unknown type, item-not-in-policy, negative amount, too-many-damages) — those are later todos. Only quote + claim happy path.
Current error: expected 0 to be 100
Implementation file: src/cli.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

49	28 G | Math.floor on summed total payout (per R6 final rounding) | passing:28
50	29 R | sword + amulet → insurance sum 1600, cap 3200 (remainingCap 3100 after 200 dmg) | comp:OK | run:OK | no comp error expected — quote/claim & fields exist, vitest no type-check; ClaimResult lacks remainingCap so result.remainingCap is undefined, expected 3100
51	29 G | add remainingCap to ClaimResult + insuranceValueByType lookup; cap=2×sum(insurance values over policy.items), remainingCap=cap−payout | passing:29

53	31 R | sword + 3 runes (block) → insurance sum 1750, cap 3500 (block discount affects premium only) | comp:OK | run:OK | no comp error expected; test passes immediately — totalInsuranceValue sums insuranceValueByType (1000+3×250=1750) independent of premiumForTypeBlock discount, cap 3500, remainingCap 3500−100=3400
54	32 R | sword (cap 2000), two successive 1500 claims → 1400 (rem 600) then 600 (rem 0) | comp:OK | run:OK | no comp error expected — quote/claim & remainingCap field exist, vitest no type-check; claim recomputes remainingCap=2000−payout fresh each call with no cross-claim cap memory, so 2nd claim returns payout 1400 again, expected 600
55	32 G | store remainingCap on policy (lazy-init full cap), payout=min(desired,remainingBefore), decrement & persist | passing:32
56	33 R | two swords → insurance sum 2000, cap 4000 (remainingCap 3900 after 200 dmg) | comp:OK | run:OK | no comp error expected; test passes immediately — totalInsuranceValue reduces over all policy.items (1000+1000=2000), cap=2×2000=4000, payout 200−100=100, remainingCap 3900 matches; multiple same-type items already summed by existing reduce
57	34 R | two swords both damaged (two sword entries) → each own 100 G deductible | comp:OK | run:OK | no comp error expected; test passes immediately — cycle-27 reduce-over-all-damages applies per-entry deductible, (500−100)+(500−100)=800, cap 4000 not binding
58	35 R | CLI reads stdin JSON, writes {results:[{premium}]}; amulet 5yr → premium 59 | comp:OK | run:OK | comp pred = no TS error (spec spawns tsx, no static import, vitest no type-check); missing src/cli.ts surfaced as runtime ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND, then stub wrote premium:0 for clean assertion-stage failure (exp 59)
59	35 G | CLI maps steps to quote(customer,items,{followUp:false}) returning {premium}; no claim/validation | passing:35
60	36 R | CLI writes payout+remainingCap for claim step (amulet quote then amulet dmg200 → payout 100, remainingCap 1100) | comp:OK | run:OK | comp pred = no TS error (spec spawns tsx, no static import, vitest no type-check); CLI ran quote on claim step's missing items → TypeError reduce-of-undefined, then stub returned {payout:0,remainingCap:0} for claim op → clean assertion-stage failure (exp 100)
61

1	# Example Mapping: MHPCO Claim Office
2	
3	Feature file: `src/claim-office.ts` (core logic) + `src/cli.ts` (CLI entry).
4	Tests: `src/claim-office.spec.ts`.
5	
6	Two operations: **quote** (compute premium for a list of items) and **claim**
7	(process a damage report against a policy created by an earlier quote).
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Rules
12	
13	### R1 — Item base premiums and insurance values
14	Price list per main item:
15	- Sword: insurance value 1000 G, base premium 100 G
16	- Amulet: 600 G / 60 G
17	- Staff: 800 G / 80 G
18	- Potion: 400 G / 40 G
19	
20	Components (rune, moonstone, ...): insurance value 250 G each, base premium
21	25 G per component.
22	
23	### R2 — Building block of 3 alike components
24	A block of exactly 3 alike components costs 60 G base premium (instead of
25	3×25 = 75). Block requires **exactly 3**; surplus components are billed at
26	25 G each. Greedily form as many 3-blocks as possible per type.
27	
28	### R3 — "Alike" = same type
29	A block is formed only from components of the **same type**. Different types
30	(rune vs moonstone) form separate blocks; they do not combine.
31	
32	### R4 — Premium modifiers
33	- Cursed item: +50 % surcharge (item-specific, on the item's base premium)
34	- High enchantment (enchantment level ≥ 5): +30 % surcharge (item-specific)
35	- Loyalty (≥ 2 years with MHPCO): −20 % discount (policy-wide)
36	- First insurance: +10 % surcharge (policy-wide — see R8)
37	- Follow-up contract (each contract after the first): −15 % discount (policy-wide)
38	- Processing fee: +5 G, added at the very end (after everything)
39	
40	### R5 — Modifier scope
41	- Item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the **affected
42	  item's base premium** only.
43	- Policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up contract) apply
44	  to the **policy base premium** = sum of all item base premiums.
45	- The processing fee is added **last**.
46	
47	Worked policy total order:
48	`policyTotal = sum(itemBase + itemSpecificSurcharges)`
49	then apply policy-wide percentage modifiers, then `+ 5 G fee`.
50	
51	### R6 — Rounding in MHPCO's favor
52	- Premium → rounded **up** to whole G (ceil).
53	- Payout → rounded **down** to whole G (floor).
54	- Intermediate amounts are kept as exact fractions; only the final premium /
55	  payout is rounded.
56	
57	### R7 — Claim processing
58	- 100 G deductible per damage **event** (= per damaged item / per entry in
59	  `damages`).
60	- Total payout per policy capped at **2 × insurance sum** (based on unmodified
61	  insurance values; premium modifiers do not raise the cap).
62	- High enchantment (≥ 8) on the damaged item → reimbursed at 50 % of the
63	  damage amount (the 50 % applies **before** the deductible).
64	- Dragon material → fully reimbursed.
65	- When both the ≥ 8 clause and dragon clause apply, the 50 % rule wins.
66	- Deductible applies after the percentage clause.
67	- Cap is shared and exhausted across successive claims on the same policy;
68	  a payout is reduced to the remaining cap.
69	
70	### R8 — First insurance semantics (clarified ❓)
71	"First insurance" surcharge applies to **every item in a quote**, regardless
72	of customer history. It is NOT tied to customer's first-ever contract nor to
73	whether we've seen the item before. (Modeled as a policy-wide +10 % here,
74	consistent with the integration example arithmetic.)
75	
76	### R9 — CLI
77	- Reads a JSON scenario from stdin: `{ customer: { yearsWithMHPCO }, steps: [...] }`.
78	- Steps processed sequentially; `claim` steps reference an earlier quote step
79	  by zero-based index in `policy`.
80	- Writes `{ results: [...] }` to stdout, same length/order as `steps`.
81	  - quote result: `{ premium: <int> }`
82	  - claim result: `{ payout: <int>, remainingCap: <int> }`
83	- Follow-up contract discount: a quote step is "first" if it's the customer's
84	  first quote in the scenario; later quotes get the −15 %.
85	
86	### R10 — Error / edge conditions (CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr)
87	- Unknown item type in a quote (e.g. `broomstick`).
88	- Claim references an item not in the policy (wrong type, or unknown type).
89	- Damage entry with negative `amount`.
90	- `damages` contains more entries of a type than the policy covers
91	  (e.g. two sword damages, one sword insured) → whole claim rejected.
92	- Empty item list is NOT an error → premium 5 G (fee only).
93	
94	---
95	
96	## Examples (with expected values)
97	
98	### Base premiums / blocks (R1, R2, R3)
99	- 2 runes → 50 G base (2×25)
100	- 3 runes → 60 G base (block)
101	- 4 runes → 100 G base (block of 3 + 1 single = 60 + 25)... NOTE spec says
102	  "4 runes → 100 G (no block — block requires exactly 3)". So 4 runes = 100 G,
103	  i.e. 4×25, NOT 60+25. Block applies only when count is exactly a multiple
104	  of 3? Re-read: "7 runes → 175 G". 7 = 175. 175 = 7×25. And 3→60, 6→? Spec
105	  only gives 3→60. For 7: if greedy blocks, 2 blocks (6) + 1 = 120+25 = 145,
106	  but spec says 175 = 7×25. So the block applies ONLY when the count is
107	  EXACTLY 3. Any other count → all components billed singly at 25 G each.
108	  - 2 runes → 50, 3 runes → 60, 4 runes → 100 (4×25), 7 runes → 175 (7×25).
109	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 75 G (no block; 3×25, different types) (R3)
110	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120 G (two separate blocks, 60+60) (R3)
111	
112	### Modifier scope (R5)
113	- Policy: cursed sword (base 100) + plain amulet (base 60) → policy base 160;
114	  cursed surcharge = 50 (50 % of sword's 100, not of 160) → 210 before
115	  policy-wide modifiers and fee.
116	
117	### Modifier thresholds (R4)
118	- Exactly 2 years → loyalty applies.
119	- Sword enchantment exactly 5 → high-enchantment applies.
120	- Sword enchantment 4 → no high-enchantment.
121	- Dragon sword, enchantment exactly 8, damage 1000 → payout 400
122	  (≥8 clause: 50 % → 500; then deductible 100 → 400).
123	
124	### Claim clauses (R7)
125	- Dragon attack: insured sword damaged 500 + insured amulet damaged 300 →
126	  payout 600 (deductible once per item: (500−100)+(300−100)=400+200=600).
127	- Regular sword (steel, ench 3), damage 500 → 400 (500−100).
128	- Rune (value 250), damage 200 → 100 (200−100; no clauses for components).
129	- Dragon sword, ench 9, damage 1000 → 400 (50 % wins: 500, −100).
130	- Dragon sword, ench 5, damage 800 → 700 (dragon full: 800, −100).
131	- Steel sword, ench 9, damage 1000 → 400 (50 %: 500, −100).
132	
133	### Multiple same-type items (R7, R10)
134	- Two swords → insurance sum 2000, cap 4000.
135	- Dragon attack damages both swords (two sword entries) → each its own
136	  deductible.
137	- More sword damages than swords insured → CLI non-zero exit, claim rejected.
138	
139	### Cap (R7)
140	- Sword + amulet → insurance sum 1600, cap 3200.
141	- Cursed sword (value 1000, premium 165) → cap 2000 (unmodified value).
142	- Sword + 3 runes (block) → insurance sum 1750 (1000 + 3×250); block
143	  discount affects premium only, not sum.
144	- Sword (sum 1000, cap 2000), two successive claims of 1500 each:
145	  - claim 1 → payout 1400 (1500−100), remaining cap 600.
146	  - claim 2 → desired 1400 reduced to remaining 600 → payout 600, remaining 0.
147	
148	### Rounding (R6)
149	- Premium 197.5 → 198 (up).
150	- Payout 350.5 → 350 (down).
151	
152	### Edge cases (R10)
153	- Empty item list → premium 5 (fee only).
154	- Unknown item type in quote → non-zero exit, stderr, no results on stdout.
155	- Claim references item not in policy → non-zero exit.
156	- Damage amount −200 → non-zero exit.
157	
158	### Integration (R4, R5, R8)
159	- Newcomer (0 yrs, no previous contract), cursed sword (steel, ench 3) →
160	  premium 165. (100 base + 50 curse + 10 first insurance = 160; + 5 fee = 165.)
161	  Arithmetic: itemSpecific → 100 + 50 = 150 (curse). policy-wide: first
162	  insurance +10 % of policy base 100 = +10 → 160; fee +5 → 165.
163	  NOTE: the +10 first-insurance is 10 % of the policy base premium (100),
164	  giving 10, applied to the surcharged total 150 → 160. Confirmed by numbers.
165	- Long-standing customer's 2nd contract (3 yrs), cursed sword (steel, ench 7)
166	  → premium 160.
167	  (100 base + 50 curse + 30 high ench − 20 loyalty + 10 first ins − 15
168	  follow-up = 155; + 5 fee = 160.)
169	  Each %-modifier computed on the policy base premium 100:
170	  curse 50 (on item base 100), high ench 30 (on item base 100), loyalty
171	  −20 (20 % of 100), first insurance +10 (10 % of 100), follow-up −15 (15 %
172	  of 100). Item base 100 + 50 + 30 − 20 + 10 − 15 = 155; + 5 = 160.
173	  => All percentage modifiers are computed against the relevant base (item
174	  base for item-specific, policy base premium for policy-wide), then summed
175	  additively, not compounded. This matches both integration examples.
176	
177	---
178	
179	## Interface contract (verbatim field names — binding)
180	
181	**Input (stdin):**
182	```json
183	{
184	  "customer": { "yearsWithMHPCO": 5 },
185	  "steps": [
186	    { "op": "quote",
187	      "items": [ { "type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false } ] },
188	    { "op": "claim",
189	      "policy": 0,
190	      "incident": { "cause": "fire", "damages": [ { "itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200 } ] } }
191	  ]
192	}
193	```
194	
195	**Output (stdout):**
196	```json
197	{ "results": [ { "premium": 65 }, { "payout": 100, "remainingCap": 1100 } ] }
198	```
199	(amulet premium for 5-yr customer, ench 2, not cursed: base 60; loyalty −20 %
200	= −12, first insurance +10 % = +6, → 60 − 12 + 6 = 54; + 5 fee = 59. payout
201	200 − 100 = 100; cap = 2×600 = 1200, remaining 1100. These are illustrative;
202	the spec only fixes the shape with `<integer>` placeholders, so the CLI shape
203	test asserts structure/keys, not these exact derived numbers.)
204	
205	Item object fields: `type`, `material?`, `enchantment?`, `cursed?`.
206	Damage object fields: `itemType`, `amount`.
207	Claim step fields: `op`, `policy`, `incident{ cause, damages[] }`.
208	
209	---
210	
211	## Questions / Clarifications (❓ from spec — resolved)
212	- Q: "alike" = same type or same family? → **Same type** (R3). Separate blocks
213	  per type.
214	- Q: cursed surcharge whole policy or just cursed item? → **Just the cursed
215	  item's base premium** (R5).
216	- Q: two of the same item? → Each is a separate insured item; insurance sum
217	  adds up; each damage entry has its own deductible (R7).
218	- Q: "first insurance" = first ever contract or first time seeing item? →
219	  **Applies to every item in a quote regardless of history** (R8).
220	
221	---
222	
223	## Per-test rationale (each it.todo below)
224	1. amulet base 60 — R1 simplest single item.
225	2. sword base 100 — R1.
226	3. staff base 80 — R1.
227	4. potion base 40 — R1.
228	5. single rune base 25 — R1 component.
229	6. empty item list → 5 (fee only) — R10/R6 edge, simplest fee.
230	7. 2 runes → 50 base — R2 below-block.
231	8. 3 runes → 60 base (block) — R2.
232	9. 4 runes → 100 base (no block) — R2 exactly-3 rule.
233	10. 7 runes → 175 base — R2.

content.text: Now let me look at the failing test and the claim-office exports.

I have the full context. The `claim` function takes `(policy: QuoteResult, incident: Incident)`. I need the CLI to store the QuoteResult from quote steps and reuse it in claim steps.

Now I'll write the minimal implementation. The current claim branch is faked with `{payout: 0, remainingCap: 0}`. This test forces generalization — I must wire up real claim handling. But I'll keep it minimal: handle quote (store result, push premium) and claim (look up stored result, call claim, push payout/remainingCap).

content.text:
🟢 Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: In `src/cli.ts`, kept an array `quotedPolicies` indexed by step index. For each step: `quote` steps call `quote()`, store the returned `QuoteResult` at their step index, and push `{premium}`; `claim` steps look up the stored `QuoteResult` at `step.policy`, call `claim(policy, step.incident)`, and push `{payout, remainingCap}`. Used the `map` index argument for storage (no fragile object lookup).
**Result**: All tests now pass (36 passing, 4 todo).
**Approach**: This is minimal — the test forced generalization because the prior fake (`{payout:0, remainingCap:0}`) cannot produce 100/1100, and there is no smaller branch that satisfies it. The implementation reuses the existing `quote`/`claim` core functions (already built in earlier cycles) and the same `QuoteResult` object so its mutable `remainingCap` is preserved. No validation/error logic was added — the 4 remaining error todos (unknown type, item-not-in-policy, negative amount, too-many-damages) stay untouched, so none of them will pass prematurely when activated.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-22_10-51-43_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.2.1-fake-it-green_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts`
